 {"id":"bd-17w24","title":"Mermaid Styling Support (classDef/style/linkStyle)","description":"Background
- Mermaid diagrams use styling directives to convey semantics.
- Terminal constraints mean we map styles to fg/bg/border/accent + glyph choices.

Scope
- Parse classDef/class/style/linkStyle directives from AST/Init.
- Resolve style precedence (node-specific > class > global defaults).
- Apply init themeVariables where supported (mapped to theme tokens).
- Map styles to theme tokens and rendering primitives (fg/bg, border, line style, arrowhead style).
- Clamp to accessible contrast where possible (prevent low-contrast combos).
- Provide graceful fallback for unsupported styles with warnings (compat matrix).

Considerations
- Deterministic mapping; same styles → same tokens.
- Avoid invalid color combos; clamp to theme palette.
- Log style resolution and fallback decisions in evidence logs.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for precedence resolution + style mapping.
- Snapshot tests for styled nodes/edges across Unicode/ASCII modes.

Success Criteria
- Styles applied where supported; unsupported styles degrade predictably.","notes":"Implemented linkStyle index correctness by preserving edge order (edge_drafts sorted by insertion_idx), added test resolve_styles_linkstyle_respects_edge_order, updated docs/spec/mermaid-config.md Style Resolution section.  {"id":"bd-19cll","title":"Layout Aesthetic Metrics + Tuning Harness","description":"Background
- Layout beauty is a primary goal but needs objective, tunable metrics.

Scope
- Define aesthetic metrics: crossings, bends, edge length variance, symmetry, alignment, compactness.
- Add readability penalties for label collisions and long detours.
- Build a tuning harness to compare layouts with different weights.
- Expose metric scores in evidence logs and debug overlay.
- Provide deterministic weight presets for compact/normal/rich tiers.

Considerations
- Must remain deterministic; no stochastic search.
- Metrics should be cheap enough for interactive use.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for metric computations on known layouts.
- Snapshot tests to ensure preset changes do not regress visuals.

Success Criteria
- Metrics available for decision-making and debugging.
- Presets produce visibly improved layouts at larger sizes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:56:08.216619699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:24:16.268506554Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T06:24:16.268443105Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-19cll","depends_on_id":"bd-2fp5r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:56:13.452960628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-19cll","depends_on_id":"bd-2hxl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:56:19.088240976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1k7c5","title":"Perf: Profile + Optimize Layout/Render Hotspots","description":"Background

- Must follow extreme optimization loop: baseline → profile → single lever → verify.

- Baseline runtime with hyperfine for large diagrams.
- Profile hotspots (cargo flamegraph or perf).
- Opportunity matrix; implement only score ≥ 2.0 changes.
- Verify deterministic output via golden checksums.
- Track memory allocations and cache hit rates.

Considerations
- One lever per change; no behavior drift.
- Capture before/after metrics in comments or logs.

Success Criteria
- Measured speedup without output changes.

 {"id":"bd-1rzr","title":"Action Timeline: Alert Threshold Calibration (Conformal + e-process)","description":"## Goal
Set change-point alert thresholds with formal false-positive control and provide an explanation ledger per alert.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Conformal thresholding on residuals from the sketch baseline:
- Given calibration residuals R = {r_1..r_n}, threshold q = quantile_{1-alpha} using (n+1) rule.
- Alert when r_t > q.

Anytime-valid layer:
- Maintain an e-process on residuals to allow early alerts without inflating false positives.
- Example: e_t = exp(lambda * (r_t - mu) - (lambda^2 * sigma^2)/2), E_t = product e_t.
- Trigger if E_t > 1/alpha.

## Acceptance Criteria
- FPR <= alpha on synthetic and recorded traces.
- Alert carries evidence ledger: residual, q, conformal score, e-value, alpha.
- No alert spam under steady-state traffic.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_conformal_threshold: threshold equals (1-alpha) quantile of calibration set.
- unit_evalue_monotone: e-process nonnegative and grows with evidence.
- property_fpr_control: empirical FPR <= alpha + tolerance.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Replay trace with known change points; log alert timing and evidence.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-2bzl","title":"Text Effects: AsciiArtText Integration","description":"## Background
AsciiArtText exists but doesn't fully integrate with the effect system. Multi-line text needs:
- Consistent effect application across all rows
- Reflection support (already partly addressed)
- Gradient mapping that makes sense for 2D text

## Implementation

### StyledText from AsciiArt:
```rust
impl StyledText {
    /// Create StyledText from AsciiArtText for multi-line rendering
    pub fn from_ascii_art(art: AsciiArtText) -> StyledMultiLine {
        StyledMultiLine {
            lines: art.render_lines(),
            effects: Vec::new(),
            // ...
        }
    }
}

/// Multi-line text with effects
pub struct StyledMultiLine {
    lines: Vec<String>,
    effects: Vec<TextEffect>,
    easing: Easing,
    time: f64,
    reflection: Option<Reflection>,
}
```

### Effect Application for 2D:
When applying effects to multi-line text:
- Gradients use 2D coordinates (x=char, y=row)
- Wave can affect y-offset per row
- Glow/Shadow renders around entire block

### Render Method:
```rust
impl Widget for StyledMultiLine {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame) {
        for (row_idx, line) in self.lines.iter().enumerate() {
            let row_y = area.y + row_idx as u16;
            // Apply effects with 2D context
            self.render_line(line, area.x, row_y, row_idx, self.lines.len(), frame);
        }
        
        // Render reflection if present
        if let Some(ref reflection) = self.reflection {
            self.render_reflection(area, frame, reflection);
        }
    }
}
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_ascii_art_to_styled: Conversion preserves lines
- test_multiline_gradient_2d: Gradient uses x and y
- test_multiline_effects_apply: Each line gets effects
- test_multiline_with_reflection: Reflection renders below

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot 5-line 'HELLO' with vertical gradient
- Snapshot same with reflection

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: AsciiArtText Integration
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-2nkmi","title":"Mermaid Showcase Super Screen (over-the-top interactive demo)","description":"Purpose
- Build a new, over-the-top interactive demo screen that makes the Mermaid pipeline visible and impressive: parse -> IR -> layout -> render, with live controls and performance instrumentation.
- Demonstrate fast, deterministic recomputation of "optimal" layouts across many complex samples.

User Value / Why
- Turns the existing mermaid capabilities in ftui-extras into a flagship, testable, keyboard-first demo.
- Gives clear evidence of correctness, determinism, and performance budgets.

Scope
- New screen in ftui-demo-showcase with rich UI (header, sample list, viewport, controls, metrics, status log).
- Large curated sample library spanning multiple Mermaid diagram types and edge cases.
- Interactive controls for layout/fidelity/viewport (including viewport size overrides) and a metrics panel showing timing + quality.

Non-Goals
- No full Mermaid editor; samples are read-only.
- No backward compat shims; update core code directly if needed.

Acceptance Criteria
- Screen renders complex Mermaid examples deterministically for fixed inputs.
- Layout/fidelity/viewport changes visibly update output and metrics.
- Metrics panel shows parse/layout/render times and layout quality fields.
- Works at 80x24 (minimal), 120x40 (default), 200x60 (max showcase).
- Unit tests, snapshots, and e2e harness runs validate behavior with detailed logs.

Testing Requirements (must be included by child tasks)
- Unit tests: state transitions, keybindings, rendering triggers, error handling.
- Snapshot tests: 3+ sizes x 3+ diagram types, with metrics visibility + viewport size overrides.
- E2E scripts: deterministic seed, exit-after, JSONL log validation, clear failure messages.

 {"id":"bd-2nkmi.1","title":"Spec the Mermaid showcase screen UX + interaction model","description":"What (spec already drafted in docs/spec/mermaid-showcase.md)
- This bead captures the UX + interaction model and acts as source of truth for implementation.

Key UX decisions (summary, keep in-sync with spec file)
- Screen regions: header (title, sample index, layout, fidelity, status), sample list, render viewport, control panel, metrics panel, footer hints.
- Layout rules by size:
  - 80x24: collapsed sample selector, compact metrics line, control overlay toggle.
  - 120x40: 3-column layout (sample list | viewport | controls+metrics).
  - 200x60: expanded layout with optional status log panel.
- Keybindings (required set):
  - Sample nav: Up/Down or j/k, Home/End, Enter (re-render)
  - Zoom: +/-, 0 reset, f fit
  - Viewport size override: [ / ] adjust width, { / } adjust height (or equivalent explicit mapping)
  - Layout: l cycle mode (Auto/Dense/Spacious), r force re-layout
  - Panels: m toggle metrics, c toggle controls
  - Fidelity: t cycle tier, g glyph mode, s styles, w wrap mode
  - Help: ? overlay, Esc close overlay
- State model: selected_index, layout_mode, fidelity, glyph_mode, wrap_mode, styles_enabled, metrics_visible, controls_visible,
  viewport_zoom/pan, viewport_size_override (w,h), last_render report, last_error.
- Metrics fields: parse_ms, layout_ms, render_ms, layout_iterations, objective_score, constraint_violations, fallback_tier, fallback_reason.

Acceptance
- Spec is self-contained and includes the above details; no external plan needed.
